name: itsolpowers
description: ITSOL engineering skills and bounded subagents with configurable workflow modes and a cost-aware execution policy for bounded models, reasoning, delegation, review, evidence-based completion without maxTurns, and ranked stop stages, plus engineering domain workflows.

interface.shortDescription: ITSOL workflow modes, planning, TDD, current docs, implementation, and review.
interface.longDescription: ITSOL Powers provides Codex skills for routing engineering tasks through the central itsol-workflow-mode contract: governed approvals, autonomous-planned reviewed artifacts without approval pauses, or direct execution without persistent planning artifacts. It preserves task-scoped authority, honest Draft, Approved, Ready for execution, and not-required states, repository defaults and restrictions, TDD or replacement verification, separate authorization for protected actions, and explicit setup/diagnostics for cost-aware Codex subagent roles. The plugin also covers repo memory and monorepo policy, current technology research, ML/AI, UI/UX, React 19 and Next.js, TanStack Query, agent-browser QA, Expo, Electron, Tauri, generated OpenAPI clients, migrations, SQL Server/.NET data access, databases, subagent execution, debugging, self-review, security, infrastructure, deployment readiness, and incident analysis.
defaultPrompt: Use ITSOL Powers to classify this task and choose the right skills.

Use itsol-workflow-mode before planning or implementation to resolve governed, autonomous-planned, or direct mode from platform rules, repository restrictions, an explicit task-level choice, repository default, then governed fallback.

Use itsol-execution-policy after workflow mode to resolve separate model/reasoning intent, distinct-agent and parallel ceilings, review cycles, done_when, ranked stop_after, and escalation behavior; never use maxTurns or infer completion from agent termination.
Use $itsol-codex-setup to preview and install managed economy, balanced, or quality Codex roles, and $itsol-codex-doctor to diagnose them without changing files.

Preserve the selected workflow mode for the current task and use honest artifact states: Draft then Approved in governed, Draft then Ready for execution in autonomous-planned, and not-required in direct.

If .itsol.md exists, read its repo memory before choosing project-specific TDD, verification, workflow-mode, or monorepo policy; create, initialize, or update it only when explicitly requested or otherwise authorized by the selected workflow scope.
When planning or reviewing framework, SDK, runtime, package, generated client, or API-dependent work, verify current official documentation and repo-pinned versions first.

For machine learning and AI engineering work, route through ML project planning, data/evaluation, training/experiments, LLM/RAG, or serving/MLOps/review skills; prefer rust-ml-llm skills first for Rust/Rig/Candle-specific code.
For React 19 and Next.js work, route through React/Next implementation, review, debugging, App Router/rendering, API/cache/forms, or quality/security skills as appropriate.

For TanStack Query v5 in React/Next.js, use focused implementation, review, or debugging skills for query keys, SSR hydration, mutations, invalidation, optimistic updates, auth cache, tenant isolation, and tests.

For agent-browser local dogfood, browser-based reproduction, evidence capture, or pre-QA validation, route through agent-browser workflow, interaction debugging, diagnostics/evidence, QA reporting, or security/production safety skills; check the installed CLI version/help and load local core/dogfood guidance when the CLI supports it.

For Expo React Native mobile app work, route through Expo implementation, review, debugging, security/permissions, or EAS release/OTA skills for Expo SDK, Expo Router, development builds, CNG/prebuild, app config, permissions, storage/offline, native modules, EAS Build, EAS Submit, EAS Update, runtime versions/channels, OTA rollout/rollback, CI, store release, and mobile diagnostics.

For Electron desktop app work, route through Electron implementation, review, debugging, security hardening, or release/distribution skills for main/preload/renderer boundaries, IPC, BrowserWindow, storage, auto-update, packaging, signing, and packaged-app diagnostics.

For Tauri desktop app work, route through Tauri implementation, review, debugging, security/capabilities, or release/distribution skills for Rust/frontend boundaries, commands, invoke/events, capabilities, permissions, WebView differences, sidecars, updater, bundling, signing, and platform diagnostics.
For visible frontend work, use UI/UX skills for design-system consistency, component architecture, view states, responsiveness, accessibility, performance, tests, and UI review.
For application rewrites or technology migrations, inventory behavior, choose a safe migration strategy, and plan incremental slices before implementation.

In governed mode, require reviewed Business and Technical Plans plus explicit approval of each specific artifact; in autonomous-planned mode, review plans and continue with Ready for execution; in direct mode, do not require persistent planning artifacts.

When execution policy permits subagents, split only independent work, stay within distinct-agent/concurrency/review ceilings, and validate each response; if required review does not fit, return partial or blocked instead of weakening it.
For bugfixes, always gather evidence; apply the mode-specific Technical Fix Plan state from itsol-workflow-mode instead of an unconditional approval gate.
Use ITSOL TDD workflow before changing production code.
Review this change against ITSOL security and infrastructure practices.
Debug this issue using ITSOL task and incident workflows.
